question: Under the Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960, what type of insurance is compulsory for every motor car user in Singapore?
explanation: The Act requires every motor car user to have insurance covering legal liabilities for death or bodily injuries to third parties. This minimum is the Act Liability Only policy.
jpComparison: Japan: CALI (jibaiseki) similarly covers bodily injury to third parties only.

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four main types of Private Motor Car Insurance in Singapore?
explanation: The four types are: Act Liability Only, Third-Party Only, Third-Party Fire & Theft, and Comprehensive.
jpComparison: Japan: Motor insurance is structured as compulsory (jibaiseki) plus voluntary (nin-i hoken).

question: What does an Act Liability Only policy cover?
choices: Death or bodily injury to third parties including passengers
Death or bodily injury to third parties and property damage
Damage to the insured motor car only
Bodily injury to the driver and passengers
explanation: Act Liability Only covers only liability for death or bodily injuries to third parties (including passengers).
jpComparison: Japan: CALI also covers bodily injury only, not property damage.

question: What additional perils does Third-Party, Fire & Theft cover compared to Third-Party Only?
choices: Accidental damage to the insured car
Fire and theft damage to the insured car
explanation: Third-Party, Fire & Theft extends Third-Party Only by protecting the insured car against fire or theft.
jpComparison: Japan: Japanese voluntary motor insurance can include fire and theft as selected perils.

question: For which type of car is Third-Party, Fire & Theft usually purchased?
choices: Brand new cars under hire purchase
Older cars, e.g. above 10 years of age
explanation: Usually purchased for older cars (above 10 years), though premium may not be significantly less.
jpComparison: Japan: Owners of older vehicles similarly choose limited coverage to save on premiums.

question: Which type of Private Motor Car Insurance provides the broadest protection?
explanation: Comprehensive offers the broadest protection including own damage, liability, medical, and PA benefits.
jpComparison: Japan: Full-coverage voluntary insurance with vehicle coverage is the closest equivalent.

question: When is Comprehensive motor insurance a requirement in Singapore?
choices: When the car purchase is financed by a loan
explanation: Comprehensive cover is required when a car is financed by a loan (hire purchase).
jpComparison: Japan: Auto loans similarly require comprehensive coverage.

question: Which motor insurance document must be shown to the police as evidence of insurance?
explanation: The Certificate of Insurance must be shown to police as and when required.
jpComparison: Japan: The CALI certificate must similarly be carried in the vehicle.

question: Which is NOT covered under Section I (Own Damage) of a Comprehensive motor policy?
explanation: Section I excludes depreciation/wear and tear, consequential loss, mechanical/electrical breakdown, and tyre-only damage.
jpComparison: Japan: Vehicle insurance similarly excludes depreciation and wear and tear.

question: Under Section II, is the indemnity limit for death or bodily injury to third parties capped?
choices: Yes, at S$1 million per person
explanation: As required under the Act, indemnity for death or bodily injury to third parties is unlimited.
jpComparison: Japan: CALI has capped limits while voluntary cover is unlimited.

question: Under Section IV, what is the death benefit payable to the insured?
explanation: Section IV death benefit for the insured is S$20,000.
jpComparison: Japan: CALI pays up to 30 million yen for death.

question: What is the NCD entitlement after one claim-free year?
explanation: NCD scale: Year 1=10%, Year 2=20%, Year 3=30%, Year 4=40%, Year 5+=50%.
jpComparison: Japan: NCD uses a 1-20 grade scale rather than percentage per year.

question: What is the maximum NCD percentage achievable?
explanation: Maximum NCD is 50% after 5+ consecutive claim-free years.
jpComparison: Japan: Maximum discount is 63% at Grade 20.

question: What does the Motor Insurers Bureau (MIB) cover?
choices: Hit-and-run and claims against uninsured drivers
Only property damage from uninsured drivers
explanation: MIB compensates victims of accidents caused by uninsured or untraced motorists.
jpComparison: Japan: Government guarantee scheme serves a similar function.

question: What does BOLA stand for?
explanation: BOLA = Barometer Of Liability Agreement, replaced KFKA on 13 May 2003.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar fault determination guidelines exist.

question: The MCF requires motor accidents to be reported within what timeframe?
explanation: Under the MCF, accidents must be reported within 24 hours or by the next working day (Ch1 §7.7). The 'next working day' qualifier is important for accidents occurring on weekends or public holidays.
jpComparison: Japan: No strict 24-hour deadline but prompt notification required.

question: An excess (deductible) means the insured bears which portion of a claim?
choices: Any amount above the policy limit
explanation: Excess is the first part of any claim the insurer will not pay.
jpComparison: Japan: Excess (mensakukin) works identically.

question: Which agreement replaced KFKA on 13 May 2003?
explanation: BOLA replaced the Knock-for-Knock Agreement on 13 May 2003.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar knock-for-knock arrangements have evolved.

question: Under Section 2 of the Motor Vehicles Act 1960, road is defined as:
choices: Any public road and any other road to which the public has access, including bridges
explanation: Road means any public road and any other road to which the public has access, including bridges.
jpComparison: Japan: The Road Traffic Act also defines roads broadly.

question: Why is Act Liability Only generally not sold as separate cover?
choices: Most accidents involve vehicle damage so motorists opt for broader coverage
explanation: Most accidents involve vehicle damage, so motorists choose broader coverage.
jpComparison: Japan: Most drivers also purchase voluntary insurance beyond CALI.

question: Which is correct about Third-Party Only motor insurance?
choices: Covers death/bodily injury to third parties and third-party property damage
Only covers death or bodily injury
Provides medical expenses for the insured
explanation: Covers death/bodily injury AND third-party property damage, not the insured car.
jpComparison: Japan: Voluntary third-party liability similarly covers both.

question: What must the insured do if the Certificate of Insurance is lost?
choices: File a police report in 24 hours
explanation: A statutory declaration must be made.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar re-issuance process applies.

question: Legal status of a cover note vs Certificate of Insurance?
explanation: A cover note has the same legal force.
jpComparison: Japan: Temporary certificates similarly serve as valid proof.

question: The policy document in motor insurance is best described as:
choices: Evidence of the insurance contract with terms and conditions
explanation: Evidence of the contract, not the contract itself.
jpComparison: Japan: Insurance policy similarly serves as evidence.

question: Endorsements can include which excluded perils?
choices: Flood, strike, riot, and civil commotion
explanation: Can add flood, strike, riot, and civil commotion.
jpComparison: Japan: Endorsements similarly modify standard terms.

question: Under Section I, max authorised repair cost without insurer approval?
explanation: S$300 after deduction of Own Damage Excess.
jpComparison: Japan: Typically requires insurer approval for all repairs.

question: Typical third-party property damage limit under Section II?
explanation: S$5 million per claim or series of claims from one event.
jpComparison: Japan: Commonly offers unlimited property damage cover.

question: An authorised driver under Section II is:
choices: Someone with owner permission but not named in Schedule
explanation: Has permission to drive but not named in the policy Schedule.
jpComparison: Japan: Distinguishes named and other drivers with different premiums.

question: Named drivers allowed without extra premium?
choices: Up to two qualified family members or partners
explanation: Up to two named drivers who are qualified family members or partners.
jpComparison: Japan: Allowances vary by insurer and driver age.

question: Typical medical expense limit per person per accident under Section III?
explanation: S$300 to S$1,000 per person per accident.
jpComparison: Japan: Covers actual expenses up to policy limits.

question: Section IV benefit for total loss of sight in one eye (insured)?
explanation: One eye = S$10,000; both eyes = S$20,000.
jpComparison: Japan: PA benefits use disability grades rather than fixed amounts.
source: Ch1 §4.17, Scale of Compensation (v), p.14 / Appendix 1B Scale (v), p.46

question: Under Section IV, passengers entitled to what proportion vs insured?
explanation: Passengers get half the Scale of Compensation amounts.
jpComparison: Japan: PA riders typically cover all occupants equally.
source: Ch1 §4.20, p.14 / Appendix 1B Sec IV §1(b), p.45

question: Which is an exclusion under Section IV?
choices: Self-inflicted injury, suicide, or attempted suicide
explanation: Excludes self-inflicted injury, suicide, intoxication, pregnancy/childbirth.
jpComparison: Japan: PA insurance also excludes self-inflicted injuries.
source: Ch1 §4.22, p.15 / Appendix 1B Sec IV §2(a), p.45

question: Multiple policies same insurer - Section IV benefits from:
explanation: Only entitled to payment from one policy.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar anti-duplication rules apply.
source: Ch1 §4.19, p.14 / Appendix 1B Sec IV §1 note, p.45

question: If occupants exceed legal seating capacity, Section IV benefits?
choices: No benefits or rateable proportion only
explanation: Some insurers pay nothing; others pay rateable proportion only.
jpComparison: Japan: PA riders may reduce benefits if exceeded.

question: NCD after 3 consecutive claim-free years?
jpComparison: Japan: Grade system improves 1 grade per claim-free year.

question: 50% NCD with one claim - NCD at renewal?
explanation: 50% with one claim drops to 20%.
jpComparison: Japan: One claim causes a 3-grade drop.

question: 30% NCD with one claim - NCD at renewal?
explanation: 30% with one claim drops to Nil (0%).
jpComparison: Japan: Lower levels face steeper drops.
source: Ch1 §5.10 Table 1.2 (30% or less, 1 claim → Nil), p.18

question: What is the Certificate of Merit (COM) discount?
explanation: 5% discount for 3 consecutive demerit-free years.
jpComparison: Japan: Gold Licence discount similarly rewards safe driving.

question: Geographical coverage of a Singapore motor policy?
choices: Singapore, West Malaysia, and within 80.5 km of Thailand border
explanation: Singapore, West Malaysia, and within 80.5 km of the Thailand-Malaysia border. Note: the Appendix 1B specimen policy uses '80 km' in some versions, while the textbook narrative (§5.26) specifies '80.5 km (50 miles)'.
jpComparison: Japan: Motor insurance covers Japan only.

question: Upon receiving a writ or summons, the insured must:
choices: Forward it to the insurer immediately
explanation: Must forward any legal process to the insurer immediately.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar immediate notification requirement.

question: Elderly, Young, Inexperienced Drivers excess applies to:
choices: Under 27, over 70, or less than 2 years driving experience
explanation: An additional excess applies to young drivers (usually under 27), elderly drivers (usually above 70), or inexperienced drivers (less than 2 years). Some insurers may use lower thresholds (e.g. under 25 or over 65), but the textbook's primary values are under 27 and above 70 (Ch1 S5.6(c), p.16).
jpComparison: Japan: Age-based rating serves similar purposes.
source: Ch1 §5.6(c) (Elderly, Young and/or Inexperienced Drivers Excess), p.16

question: How can an insurer cancel a motor policy?
explanation: The insurer may cancel the policy by giving seven days' written notice by registered letter (Ch1 §5.40(f) / Appendix 1B Condition 9(a)).
jpComparison: Japan: 30 days notice required.

question: Which is a general exclusion for the entire motor policy?
explanation: General exclusions include nuclear risks, war, and terrorism.
jpComparison: Japan: Nuclear and war exclusions are standard globally.
source: Ch1 §5.47 (Nuclear Risks General Exclusion) / Appendix 1B General Exclusion 4, p.24 / p.46

question: The windscreen extension covers:
explanation: Covers the windscreen and all window glass. The textbook notes that a glass roof or sunroof may also be included under this extension (Ch1 §5.55).
jpComparison: Japan: Part of standard vehicle damage cover.

question: What is the NCD Protector extension?
choices: Allows one at-fault claim per year without affecting NCD
explanation: Allows one at-fault claim per year without NCD impact.
jpComparison: Japan: Some insurers offer similar riders.
source: Ch1 §5.50 F4(f) NCD Protector table (One claim → 50%), p.26

question: When was the MCF introduced?
explanation: MCF introduced 1 June 2008.
jpComparison: Japan: Motor claims system evolved gradually.

question: When was the MIB established?
explanation: MIB established 22 February 1975.
jpComparison: Japan: Government guarantee scheme established 1955.

question: What is the doctrine of volenti in motor insurance?
choices: A person knowingly entering danger cannot claim for resulting injuries
explanation: A person who knowingly enters a dangerous situation cannot claim for resulting injuries.
jpComparison: Japan: Contributory negligence applies similarly.

question: Legal defence cost under Section II does NOT cover:
choices: Charge of causing death by dangerous driving
A murder charge from car use
explanation: Legal defence cover (up to S$3,000) is provided for defending any charge of causing death by driving, other than a charge of murder (Ch1 §4.12).
jpComparison: Japan: Legal expense riders also exclude intentional acts.
source: Ch1 §4.12 (Legal defence S$3,000, other than murder), p.13

question: 40% NCD with two claims - NCD at renewal?
explanation: 40% with two or more claims drops to Nil.
jpComparison: Japan: Two claims cause a 6-grade drop.
source: Ch1 §5.10 Table 1.2 (50% or less, 2 claims → Nil), p.18

question: FIDReC adjudication limit as of 1 July 2024?
jpComparison: Japan: Financial ADR has no fixed cap.
source: Ch1 §8.13 (FIDReC adjudication limit $150,000 from 1 July 2024), p.37

question: When was FIDReC established?
explanation: Established August 2005.
jpComparison: Japan: Financial ADR established 2010.

question: Usage Based Insurance (UBI) relies on:
choices: Telematics devices or apps monitoring driving behaviour
explanation: Telematics monitors speed, braking, acceleration, cornering, and driving time.
jpComparison: Japan: Telematics insurance growing with app-based scores.

question: What considerations apply to insuring electric vehicles?
choices: Battery cost, charging risks, and higher repair costs
explanation: Battery replacement cost, charging risks, and specialised repair costs.
jpComparison: Japan: EV-specific riders cover battery and charging equipment.
source: Ch1 §6.33–6.34 (Electric Cars: battery cost, repair cost), p.32

question: What is the purpose of the GIA Insurance Fraud Tip-off (GIFT) scheme?
choices: To standardise towing and storage charges after motor accidents
To reward individuals who report motor insurance fraud leading to successful prosecution
To provide free legal advice to accident victims
To track and monitor motor insurance claims electronically
explanation: GIFT (GIA Insurance Fraud Tip-off) is a scheme that rewards individuals with up to S$10,000 for reporting motor insurance fraud that leads to successful prosecution and conviction (Ch1 S7.17-7.19).
jpComparison: Japan: JAF provides standardised assistance; towing regulated separately.

question: Under Non-Injury Motor Accident Reporting Scheme:
choices: All accidents go to police first
Non-injury accidents go directly to insurers via approved centres
explanation: Under the Non-Injury Motor Accident Reporting Scheme, police reports are not required for non-injury accidents. Motorists exchange details, complete the GIA Singapore Motor Accident Report form, and submit it to their insurers. This can also be done through approved reporting centres (Ch1 §8.6–8.9).
jpComparison: Japan: All accidents require police reporting regardless of injury.
source: Ch1 §8.6–8.9 (Non-Injury Motor Accident Reporting Scheme), p.36

question: Motorcycle third-party property damage limit?
explanation: S$500,000 for motorcycles vs S$5 million for cars.
jpComparison: Japan: Motorcycle and car insurance share the same limits.
source: Ch1 §9.6 (Motorcycle Section II property damage S$500,000), p.41

question: Motorcycle overseas theft benefit?
explanation: 50% of loss up to S$2,500.
jpComparison: Japan: Motorcycle insurance rarely covers overseas theft.
source: Ch1 §9.5 (Motorcycle overseas theft 50% up to S$2,500), p.41

question: What is Maco?
choices: An online platform for purchasing motor insurance policies
An online claims-amount simulator that generates approximate motor accident damages figures
A system for automated fault determination in motor accidents
An electronic filing system for motor insurance policy renewals
explanation: Maco is an online motor accident claims simulator developed by the Singapore Courts and the Singapore Academy of Law (SAL) in March 2022. Users answer multiple-choice questions to generate an approximate damages figure for their motor accident claim (Ch1 S8.26-8.31, p.40).
jpComparison: Japan: Insurers use proprietary portals rather than industry-wide system.
source: Ch1 §8.26–8.31 (Motor Accident Claims Online – Maco), p.40

question: In the context of property insurance, which of the following best describes "property"?
choices: A) Only tangible items that can be touched
B) Every material thing or physical object that may suffer a fortuitous loss or damage by an insured peril
C) Only immoveable property such as buildings and land
D) Intangible assets such as goodwill and patents
explanation: In property insurance, "property" covers every material thing or physical object that may suffer a fortuitous loss or damage by an insured peril, including both moveable and immoveable property.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも「保険の目的」として建物と動産（家財）を区別し、物的損害を対象とする点は同様。

question: What distinguishes "Personal Property Insurance" from other property insurance?
choices: A) It covers only moveable property
B) It covers property belonging to or associated with individuals and families, as opposed to business property
C) It covers only immoveable property such as buildings
D) It only covers property located within Singapore
explanation: Personal Property Insurance covers property, whether moveable or immoveable, belonging to or associated with individuals and families, as opposed to property associated with a business.
jpComparison: 日本でも個人向け火災保険（住宅火災保険）と事業用火災保険は明確に区分されている。

question: The HDB Fire Insurance Scheme is compulsory for every HDB homeowner who took a mortgage loan after which date?
explanation: The HDB Fire Insurance Scheme is compulsory for every HDB homeowner with a mortgage loan taken after 1 September 1994.
jpComparison: 日本では住宅ローン利用時に火災保険加入が実質必須だが、法律上の強制保険ではない。シンガポールのHDB Fire Insuranceは法定義務である点が異なる。

question: What is the policy term of the HDB Fire Insurance Scheme?
explanation: The HDB Fire Insurance Scheme is a low-cost, five years' term policy underwritten by an insurer appointed by the Housing & Development Board.
jpComparison: 日本の住宅火災保険は最長5年（2022年10月改定後）。シンガポールのHDB Fire Insuranceも同じ5年間。

question: Which of the following perils is NOT covered under the HDB Fire Insurance Scheme?
choices: D) Bursting and overflowing of water pipe
explanation: The HDB Fire Insurance Scheme specifically excludes household contents and renovations. It covers the structure only against perils like fire, lightning, earthquake, etc.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも建物のみの契約では家財の盗難は補償されない。家財は別途「家財保険」が必要。

question: Under the HDB Fire Insurance Scheme, the Average Clause:
choices: B) Applies only to claims exceeding S$10,000
D) Applies only to contents claims
explanation: The Average Clause does not apply under the HDB Fire Insurance Scheme. The HDB has pre-determined the sum insured according to apartment type.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも新価（再調達価額）ベースの場合は比例てん補が適用されないケースがある。

question: For private apartment blocks registered as Management Corporation Strata Title (MCST) property, who is legally responsible for insuring the building against fire?
explanation: For private apartments registered as MCST property, it is the legal responsibility of the Management Corporation to insure the building and common property for damage resulting from fire.
jpComparison: 日本のマンション管理組合も共用部分の火災保険に加入する義務がある。個人の専有部分は各自で付保する点が類似。

question: Which of the following is the most common personal property insurance product in Singapore?
choices: C) Buildings and Contents Combined (Packaged Household) Insurance
explanation: Buildings and Contents Combined (or Packaged Household) Insurance is the most common property insurance product, providing cover for buildings, contents, and additional sections.
jpComparison: 日本でも「住宅総合保険」が建物と家財を一括でカバーするパッケージ商品として主流。

question: Under Packaged Household Insurance, the building is customarily insured on which basis?
explanation: When an insured purchases Packaged Household Insurance to insure the building, it is customarily insured on a Named Perils basis, meaning the policy states exactly what perils are covered.
jpComparison: 日本の住宅火災保険も「列挙危険方式」（Named Perils）が基本。住宅総合保険ではオールリスクに近い。

question: Under the Buildings section of Packaged Household Insurance, damage from bursting or overflowing of domestic water tanks is typically excluded if the building is left unoccupied for more than:
explanation: Cover for bursting or overflowing of domestic water tanks excludes loss or damage occurring while the building is unfurnished or left unoccupied for more than a stipulated number of days, e.g. 60 consecutive days.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険では空き家の場合、通知義務があり告知しないと保険金が支払われないことがある。明確な日数制限はシンガポール特有。

question: Under the flood cover of Packaged Household Insurance, some policies exclude the first S$10,000 or what percentage of the claim cost for subsidence, whichever is greater?
explanation: Some policies exclude the first S$10,000 or 10% of the claim cost, whichever is the greater, for subsidence or landslip claims.
jpComparison: 日本の水災補償でも免責金額や支払割合の制限がある。例えば床上浸水の場合のみ補償対象とする条件がある。

question: Which of the following is an extension commonly provided under the Buildings section of Packaged Household Insurance?
choices: A) Coverage for motor vehicles parked in the garage
B) Architect, surveyor, and consultant fees for reinstatement
C) Coverage for business property stored at home
D) Coverage for livestock and pets
explanation: The Buildings section commonly extends to cover architect, surveyor, and consultant fees necessarily incurred in the reinstatement of the building following insured damage.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも「残存物取り片づけ費用」や「損害防止費用」などの付随費用が補償される。

question: The limit for related extensions under the Buildings section (such as alternative accommodation) is usually up to what percentage of the sum insured?
explanation: There is a limit on how much the insurer will pay for related extensions, which is usually up to a percentage such as 10% of the sum insured, or limited to a specific amount.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも臨時費用保険金は損害保険金の10〜30%（上限あり）が一般的。

question: Under the Buildings section, theft coverage typically requires:
choices: B) Actual forcible and violent entry or exit
C) The involvement of an organised crime group
D) A minimum claim amount of S$500
explanation: The Buildings section covers theft accompanied by actual forcible and violent entry or exit. Insurers may offer removal of this restriction upon payment of additional premium.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険の盗難補償では「暴力的な侵入」の要件は通常なく、盗難全般が対象。シンガポールの方が厳しい。

question: The purpose of an excess in the Buildings section of Packaged Household Insurance is to:
choices: A) Increase the premium for the policyholder
B) Eliminate small claims and confine attention to larger claims
D) Encourage policyholders to file more claims
explanation: The purpose of the excess is not to avoid paying claims, but to eliminate small claims and confine the insurer's attention to larger claims.
jpComparison: 日本でも免責金額（自己負担額）の設定があり、少額クレームを排除し保険料を抑える目的は同じ。

question: The typical excess amount for Buildings under Packaged Household Insurance ranges from:
explanation: The excess amount for Buildings is usually kept low, ranging from S$100 to S$250 for each and every claim.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険の免責金額も数千円〜数万円程度で設定可能。シンガポールの100〜250SGDは日本円で約1万〜2.5万円に相当。

question: Under the Buildings section, the basis of settlement is typically:
choices: A) Market value at time of loss
B) Cost of reinstatement without deduction for depreciation
explanation: For buildings, the basis of settlement is the cost of reinstatement without taking into account any depreciation, restoring the property to a condition when new.
jpComparison: 日本でも新価（再調達価額）ベースでの保険金支払いが主流となっている。

question: If a building is under-insured, the condition of average means that:
choices: A) The insurer will pay the full reinstatement cost regardless
B) The insurer will pro-rata the payment according to the under-insurance
C) The policy is automatically voided
D) The insured must pay an additional premium before any claim is settled
explanation: The condition of average means that if the insured under-insures, the insurer will not pay the full reinstatement cost but will pro-rata the payment according to the under-insurance.
jpComparison: 日本でも「比例てん補」の考え方があり、保険金額が保険価額に満たない場合は按分で支払われる。

question: Which of the following items is typically excluded from the definition of "household contents" under a Packaged Household Insurance policy?
choices: C) Motor vehicles, watercraft and their accessories
explanation: The definition of household contents typically excludes motor vehicles, watercraft and their accessories and spare parts, among other items.
jpComparison: 日本の家財保険でも自動車や船舶は対象外。これらは別途自動車保険・船舶保険で付保する。

question: Under Contents insurance on an All Risks basis, the insurer will compensate for:
choices: A) Only damage from named perils listed in the policy
B) Any accidental loss or damage except those specifically excluded
C) Only theft and fire damage
D) Only damage occurring inside the insured house
explanation: On an All Risks basis, the insurer compensates for any accidental loss of or damage to household contents caused by all perils, other than those specifically excluded.
jpComparison: 日本の住宅総合保険も幅広い危険を補償するが、厳密なオールリスクではなく列挙方式の拡大版。

question: The sub-limit for accidental breakage of fixed mirrors under the Contents section is typically:
explanation: Payment for cost of replacing fixed mirrors (other than hand-held mirrors) and glass tops of furniture is usually up to a specific amount, e.g. S$500. Note: the textbook qualifies this figure with 'e.g.' — actual amounts/periods may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の家財保険でもガラス破損は補償対象だが、通常は個別の上限額設定はない。

question: Under the Contents section, spoilage of frozen food coverage is commonly subject to a sub-limit of:
explanation: Spoilage of frozen food in the refrigerator due to breakdown, explosion, or power failure is commonly subject to a sub-limit, e.g. S$2,000. Note: the textbook qualifies this figure with 'e.g.' — actual amounts/periods may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも冷凍食品の腐敗は「電気的事故」として補償されることがあるが、上限額は保険会社により異なる。

question: For the spoilage of frozen food to be covered, the refrigerator must typically be less than how many years old?
explanation: Coverage for spoiled frozen food is usually subject to the refrigerator being less than six years old.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険では冷蔵庫の年数制限は通常ない。シンガポール特有の条件。

question: The temporary removal extension under Contents typically covers property removed within Singapore up to:
explanation: Temporary removal covers loss or damage to property temporarily removed from the insured house, usually within Singapore and payable up to a certain limit, e.g. not exceeding 15% of Contents sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の家財保険でも「持ち出し家財」の補償があるが、保険金額の一定割合（通常20%）が上限。

question: For temporary removal of contents to a worldwide location, the cover is typically limited to:
explanation: Some insurers provide wider geographical coverage for temporary removal, up to worldwide, but for lower limits, e.g. up to 5% of Contents sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の持ち出し家財補償は通常国内限定。海外まで対象とするシンガポールの方が範囲が広い。

question: The emergency cash relief benefit under Contents is payable when the home is deemed uninhabitable for at least:
explanation: Emergency cash relief indemnifies the insured for purchasing essential items when the insurer's appointed assessor deems the home uninhabitable for at least five days.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険の「臨時費用保険金」は居住不能日数の条件なく損害発生時に支払われることが多い。

question: Under Contents on an All Risks basis, theft is excluded if the house is left unoccupied for more than:
explanation: Under All Risks coverage for Contents, theft is excluded if the house is left unoccupied for more than a stipulated number of days, such as 60 consecutive days. Note: the textbook qualifies this figure with 'e.g.' — actual amounts/periods may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも長期空き家の場合は通知義務があるが、明確な60日ルールはない。

question: Under Contents on an All Risks basis, which of the following theft scenarios IS covered?
choices: B) Theft by a person lawfully present in the house
C) Theft with forcible entry when the house is let
D) Theft while the house is unoccupied for 90 days
explanation: When the house is lent or let, theft is excluded unless force is used to enter the house. Theft by deception and theft by persons lawfully present are excluded.
jpComparison: 日本の家財保険の盗難補償では、賃貸住宅でも通常は補償対象。「強制侵入」要件はシンガポール特有。

question: The excess for Contents claims is generally:
choices: A) Higher than the excess for Buildings claims
B) The same as the excess for Buildings claims
C) Lower than the excess for Buildings claims
D) Not applicable to Contents claims
explanation: Payments under Contents are subject to an excess but are generally much lower than the excess under Buildings, e.g. S$50 for each and every claim.
jpComparison: 日本の家財保険でも免責金額は建物保険より低く設定されることが一般的。

question: Under the Contents settlement, which items are subject to deduction for wear and tear?
explanation: Under Contents, the settlement basis is replacement cost, although it is usual to make a deduction for wear and tear for items such as clothing and household linen.
jpComparison: 日本でも衣類等の減価償却は「時価」ベースで控除されることがある。新価特約をつければ控除なし。

question: Under Contents insurance, the insurer's liability for any one valuable article (e.g. jewellery) is typically limited to:
explanation: The policy typically specifies that the insurer's liability for valuables is limited to not exceeding 5% of Contents sum insured for any one article, and one-third of Contents sum insured in total.
jpComparison: 日本の家財保険でも貴金属・美術品は1個（1組）30万円超の場合、明記物件として申告が必要。

question: The total limit for all valuables under a Contents policy is typically:
choices: C) One-third of Contents sum insured
D) Half of Contents sum insured
explanation: The policy specifies the insurer's liability for valuables is limited to one-third of Contents sum insured in total.
jpComparison: 日本では明記物件の上限は通常、家財保険金額内で個別設定。総額の1/3という割合制限はシンガポール特有。

question: The Liability to the Public section under Packaged Household Insurance covers the insured's legal liability up to a typical limit of:
explanation: Liability cover is in respect of legal liability for accidental bodily injury/death or accidental damage to property, up to a limit of liability, e.g. S$1,000,000 per occurrence.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険は無制限または1億円〜3億円が主流。シンガポールの100万SGD（約1億円）は日本と同水準。

question: Which of the following scenarios would be covered under the Liability to the Public section?
choices: A) Liability arising from the insured's profession
B) The insured's dog bites and injures a neighbour
C) Liability from the use of a motor vehicle
D) Punitive damages awarded against the insured
explanation: The policy covers accidental bodily injury to third parties, such as the insured's dog biting a neighbour. Professional liability, vehicle use liability, and punitive damages are excluded.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険でもペットによる咬傷事故は補償対象。業務上の賠償責任は対象外。

question: Which of the following is NOT excluded under the Liability to the Public section?
choices: A) Liability from the insured's business
C) A visitor slipping on the insured's wet floor
D) Liability from use of lifts or vehicles
explanation: A visitor slipping on the wet floor is a typical liability claim payable under this section. The policy excludes professional/business liability, contractual liability, vehicle/lift use, punitive damages, and alterations/repairs.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険でも来客の転倒事故は補償対象。施設賠償責任保険に近い性格。

question: Under the Personal Accident Benefits of Packaged Household Insurance, death or permanent total disablement pays what percentage of the capital sum insured?
explanation: According to the Table of Compensation, death and permanent total disablement each pay 100% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも死亡・後遺障害は保険金額の100%が基本。

question: Under the Table of Compensation in Packaged Household Insurance, loss of one limb or sight of one eye pays:
explanation: According to Table 2.1, loss of one limb or sight of one eye pays 50% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも片眼失明や1肢の喪失は後遺障害等級に応じて保険金額の一定割合（通常50〜60%）を支払う。

question: Personal Accident benefits under Packaged Household Insurance exclude all of the following EXCEPT:
choices: C) Accidental bodily injury from a named peril
explanation: Exclusions include pre-existing defects, self-inflicted injury, pregnancy/childbirth, disease, hazardous sports, and intoxication. Accidental bodily injury from an insured peril is covered.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも既往症・自傷行為・妊娠は免責。事故による身体傷害のみ補償する点は同じ。

question: Medical expenses under Packaged Household Insurance are typically limited to:
explanation: The amount of indemnity for medical expenses is usually limited, e.g. S$1,000 per accident, subject to the same exclusions as specified for Personal Accident Benefits.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の通院・入院保険金は日額ベース（例：日額5,000円）で支払われ、総額上限とは異なる仕組み。

question: Under the Domestic Servant's Property section, which of the following is NOT covered?
choices: A) Clothing of the domestic servant
B) Personal effects of the domestic servant
C) Cash and currency notes belonging to the domestic servant
D) Items within the boundaries of the insured's house
explanation: The section covers the domestic servant's clothing and personal effects but does NOT cover cash, currency notes, bank notes, stamps, and stored value cards.
jpComparison: 日本では家事使用人の所持品を住宅保険で補償する制度は一般的ではない。シンガポールではメイド文化が背景にある。

question: Which of the following is a general exclusion applicable to the entire Packaged Household Insurance policy?
choices: A) Theft accompanied by forcible entry
C) Loss or damage caused by war, nuclear and radioactive contamination
explanation: General exclusions include war, nuclear and radioactive contamination risks, as well as confiscation by government, natural fermentation, and consequential losses other than loss of rent.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも戦争・核リスクは免責事由。これは世界共通の保険実務。

question: Under the policy conditions, the insured must notify the insurer of a claim within what typical time frame?
explanation: Most insurers include claim notification procedures requiring notification within a specific time frame, e.g. 7 or 14 days, otherwise the insurer will not be liable.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険では通常60日以内の通知が求められる。シンガポールの7〜14日はかなり短い。

question: Under the cancellation condition, how many days' notice is typically required?
explanation: The cancellation condition gives both insurer and insured the right to cancel by giving seven days' notice, with the insurer refunding a portion of the unused premium.
jpComparison: 日本の損害保険でも解約は可能だが、通知期間は保険会社により異なる。短期率表で返戻金を計算する点は共通。

question: If the insurer admits liability but the insured disagrees on the claim amount, the dispute is referred to:
choices: B) The Monetary Authority of Singapore
C) Arbitration in accordance with the Arbitration Act 2001
D) The Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre
explanation: If the insurer admits liability but there is a disagreement on the amount, it shall be referred to arbitration in accordance with prevailing statutory provisions, e.g. the Arbitration Act 2001.
jpComparison: 日本では損害保険の紛争解決は損保ADR（そんぽADRセンター）が利用可能。仲裁ではなく調停・あっせんが主流。

question: After the arbitrator makes an award, the insured must litigate within what time frame to hold the insurer liable for any excess amount?
explanation: The insured must litigate within three months after the arbitrator has made the award, otherwise the insurer will not be liable for any amount in excess of the award.
jpComparison: 日本の保険法では保険金請求権の消滅時効は3年。仲裁後の訴訟期限3ヶ月はシンガポール特有で非常に短い。

question: Under the expiry of claim condition, the insurer is typically not liable for any loss after:
choices: B) 6 or 12 months from the event
explanation: The insurer specifies that it will not be liable for any loss or damage, usually after 6 or 12 months from the happening, except when the claim is pending a court decision or arbitration.
jpComparison: 日本の保険法では保険金請求権の時効は3年。シンガポールの6〜12ヶ月は短い。

question: Insurers typically give a premium discount of about 15% for purchasing Packaged Household Insurance for a term of:
explanation: Insurers usually give a discount, such as 15% for three years of insurance.
jpComparison: 日本の火災保険でも長期契約割引がある。2022年以降は最長5年に短縮された。

question: A Valuable Articles Insurance policy typically provides cover on which basis?
explanation: Valuable Articles Insurance usually provides cover under an All Risks basis, protecting the insured's valuables against accidental or unforeseen losses unless a specific exclusion applies.
jpComparison: 日本の動産総合保険もオールリスクベースで高額品を補償する。

question: Under Valuable Articles Insurance, the per-item value limit under blanket coverage is typically:
explanation: Under blanket coverage, there is a per-item value limit, e.g. S$10,000. Items worth more than this limit are best covered individually (itemised coverage).
jpComparison: 日本の動産総合保険でも1品あたりの上限が設定されることがある。超過分は明記物件として個別申告が必要。

question: Which of the following is NOT a common exclusion under Valuable Articles Insurance?
choices: A) Wear and tear, gradual deterioration, inherent vice
C) Accidental loss while at a specific insured location
explanation: Accidental loss at an insured location is covered (not excluded). Common exclusions include wear and tear, moth/vermin, mechanical/electrical breakdown, and war/nuclear risks.
jpComparison: 日本の動産総合保険でも経年劣化・自然消耗・機械的故障は免責。偶然の事故のみが補償対象。

question: Under the Pairs & Sets Clause in Valuable Articles Insurance, if one vase of a pair insured for S$10,000 is broken, the insurer will pay:
choices: C) The market value of the remaining vase
D) Nothing, as the set is not completely destroyed
explanation: Under the Pairs & Sets Clause, the insurer pays a proportionate part of the sum insured. For a pair insured for S$10,000, one broken vase would receive S$5,000.
jpComparison: 日本の保険実務でもセット品の一部損害は按分払いが基本。ただし明確な「ペア条項」は一般的ではない。

question: Some insurers soften the Pairs & Sets Clause by paying the full replacement cost if the insured:
choices: B) Surrenders the undamaged article(s) to the insurer
C) Provides a valuation report from a certified appraiser
D) Agrees to a higher excess amount
explanation: Some insurers will pay the full replacement cost of the entire pair, set or unit, provided the insured agrees to surrender the undamaged article(s) to the insurer.
jpComparison: 日本では残存物の引き取り（残存物取得権）は保険会社の権利だが、ペア品の全額補償とセットにする制度は珍しい。

question: Automatic coverage for newly acquired valuable articles is typically provided at what percentage of the total sum insured for that category?
explanation: The insurer may provide automatic coverage for newly acquired articles at a percentage of the total sum insured for that category, e.g. 20% of the total sum insured for fine arts.
jpComparison: 日本の動産総合保険でも新規取得品の自動付保特約があるが、通知期間や割合は保険会社により異なる。

question: The insured must request coverage for newly acquired valuable articles within how many days of acquisition?
explanation: The insured must request coverage for new articles within a specified number of days, e.g. 30 days after acquisition, and must pay additional premium from the date acquired. Note: the textbook qualifies this figure with 'e.g.' — actual amounts/periods may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本でも動産保険の追加通知は速やかに行う必要があるが、具体的な日数制限は保険会社の約款による。

question: Under Valuable Articles Insurance, which of the following is a condition relating to alarm systems?
choices: A) The insured must install a new alarm system every year
B) The insured must activate alarm systems when premises are unattended and maintain them under a service contract
C) The insured must hire a security guard at all times
D) The insured may remove alarm systems with two weeks' notice to the insurer
explanation: The insured must activate alarm systems when premises are left unattended, maintain them regularly under a contract of service with the installing alarm company, and not remove or alter such protection without the insurer's prior consent.
jpComparison: 日本の動産保険でもセキュリティ設備の維持義務が条件に含まれることがあるが、警備会社との保守契約の要求は厳格。

question: The primary purpose of Personal Accident (PA) Insurance is to:
choices: A) Provide savings and investment benefits
B) Provide financial protection against accidental death or bodily injury
C) Cover death from natural causes such as disease
explanation: PA Insurance provides financial protection and financial burden relief to the insured against the risk of accidental death or bodily injury.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険も不慮の事故による死亡・傷害を補償する目的で加入する。生命保険とは別の保険種類。

question: A PA Insurance policy is classified as:
explanation: A PA Insurance contract is generally a benefit policy (i.e. a contract to pay a sum of money in the event of a specified contingency), irrespective of whether or not the insured sustains a financial loss.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険も定額給付方式（benefit policy）であり、実際の損害額に関わらず契約金額が支払われる。

question: Under a PA Insurance benefit policy, the insured:
choices: A) Must prove the quantum of financial loss to receive payment
B) Receives payment regardless of whether a financial loss is sustained
C) Can only claim if the injury prevents work entirely
D) Must first exhaust other insurance before claiming
explanation: As a benefit policy, there is no need to prove the quantum of loss, unlike a contract of indemnity. Payment is made based on the specified contingency occurring.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険も同様に、実損額の証明は不要。後遺障害等級に応じた定額が支払われる。

question: Which of the following is NOT a difference between PA Insurance and Life Insurance?
choices: A) PA Insurance does not cover death from natural causes
B) Life Insurance (except Term Life) offers savings or investment benefits
C) PA Insurance requires a life insurance licence to transact
D) PA Insurance does not carry any bonus or dividend reflecting investment income
explanation: PA Insurance does NOT require a life insurance licence. It does not constitute a policy of life insurance in accordance with the Insurance Act 1966, so general insurance companies can transact PA business.
jpComparison: 日本でも傷害保険は損害保険会社が引受可能。生命保険会社も第三分野として引き受ける。

question: PA Insurance can be bundled with all of the following types of insurance EXCEPT:
explanation: PA Insurance can be bundled with Motor Insurance, Packaged Household Insurance, Foreign Domestic Worker Insurance, Golfer's Insurance, and Travel Insurance. Marine Cargo Insurance is a commercial line product. Note: Marine Cargo Insurance is not explicitly listed in the PGI textbook as a type bundled with PA Insurance; this distractor is based on general insurance classification (Marine Cargo is a commercial line, not personal).
jpComparison: 日本でも傷害保険は自動車保険・旅行保険・ゴルファー保険などに付帯されることが一般的。

question: In the definition of accident under PA Insurance, the phrase "solely and directly" means:
choices: A) The accident must happen at the insured's workplace
B) The bodily injury must be proximately caused by the accident with no other contributing causes
C) The accident must be witnessed by at least two people
D) The injury must be visible externally
explanation: The phrase requires that the bodily injury shall have been caused solely and directly (proximately) by the accident, with no other causes contributing to the injury.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも「急激・偶然・外来」の3要件が必要。近因原則（proximate cause）の考え方は共通。

question: A man falls from his bicycle, lies on wet ground, catches pneumonia, and dies. Under PA Insurance, the death benefit is:
choices: A) Not payable because pneumonia is a disease
B) Payable because the proximate cause of death is the accident
C) Payable only if the policy has a disease extension
D) Subject to a 50% reduction for contributory causes
explanation: As the proximate cause of death is the accident (the fall from the bicycle), the death benefit is payable under PA Insurance, even though pneumonia was the immediate cause of death.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも、事故が原因で病気を発症し死亡した場合、近因が事故であれば保険金が支払われる。

question: A man breaks a leg in an accident and contracts an infectious disease from another patient in hospital, dying as a result. Under PA Insurance:
choices: A) The death benefit is payable because the accident led to hospitalisation
B) The death benefit is NOT payable because the infectious disease breaks the chain of causation
C) The death benefit is payable at 50% of the capital sum
D) The insurer must pay both accident and disease benefits
explanation: The death is not the sole and direct result of the accident, as the infectious disease constitutes a break in the chain of causation. The claim will not be admissible.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも因果関係が断絶した場合（院内感染等）は保険金支払いの対象外となることがある。

question: In the PA Insurance definition of accident, the term "external" means:
choices: A) The accident must occur outdoors
B) The cause is not internal (excludes existing physical defects like bursting of a blood vessel)
C) The injury must be visible on the body
D) The accident must involve an external third party
explanation: "External" means the opposite of internal - it excludes accidents caused by existing physical defects, such as the bursting of a blood vessel behind the eye.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の「外来性」要件も同義。体内の疾病に起因する事故は対象外。

question: In PA Insurance, the term "visible means" in the accident definition has the effect of excluding:
choices: B) Death caused by inhalation of fume or gas
explanation: The term "visible" excludes death caused by inhalation of fume or gas, as such causes are not visible.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では「急激・偶然・外来」の3要件で判断し、ガス中毒等は「外来」要件で議論される。

question: The death benefit under PA Insurance typically must occur within what period from the accident?
explanation: The death benefit often has a condition that death must occur within a certain period, e.g. 12 months, from the accident that gives rise to the claim. Note: the textbook qualifies this figure with 'e.g.' — actual amounts/periods may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも死亡保険金は事故日から180日以内の死亡が条件。シンガポールの12ヶ月は日本より長い。

question: The capital sum insured for death benefit under PA Insurance commonly ranges from:
explanation: Some insurers offer the option of selecting from a few levels of death benefits, with capital sum insured commonly between S$50,000 to S$500,000.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の死亡保険金額も数百万円〜数千万円が一般的で、レンジとしては同水準。

question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an extra benefit payable in addition to the death benefit?
choices: B) Compassionate cash relief benefit (e.g. S$2,000)
C) Funeral or bereavement grant (e.g. S$3,000)
D) Widow's pension benefit (e.g. S$5,000 monthly)
explanation: Extra benefits mentioned include lifestyle maintenance benefit (S$1,000), compassionate cash relief (S$2,000), and funeral/bereavement grant (S$3,000). Widow's pension is not mentioned.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険には通常このような付加給付はない。葬祭費用は別途の保険で対応する。

question: The usual definition of Permanent Total Disablement (PTD) under PA Insurance requires that the person:
choices: A) Cannot perform their specific occupation
B) Cannot engage in any business or gainful occupation of any kind
C) Has lost at least two limbs
D) Is confined to a hospital for more than 90 days
explanation: The usual PTD definition is a permanent total disablement that prevents a person from engaging in any business or gainful occupation of any kind.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の「後遺障害」は等級表に基づくが、PTDの定義は「いかなる業務にも従事できない」という広い定義。

question: A surgeon injures his hands and cannot operate but can work as a lecturer. Under the PTD definition, he is:
choices: A) Entitled to permanent total disablement benefit
B) NOT entitled to permanent total disablement benefit
C) Entitled to 50% of the capital sum insured
D) Entitled to temporary disablement benefit only if he chooses not to lecture
explanation: Under the PTD definition (unable to engage in any gainful occupation), the surgeon is not entitled to PTD benefit because he can work as a lecturer.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも「いかなる業務にも従事できない」が全損の基準。特定職業への従事不能は部分的後遺障害として扱われる。

question: According to the Table of Compensation (Table 3.1), loss of sight of both eyes pays:
explanation: According to Table 3.1, loss of sight of both eyes pays 100% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも両眼失明は後遺障害第1級（100%）として最高額が支払われる。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of sight of one eye pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of sight of one eye pays 50% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも片眼失明は後遺障害第8級（34%）〜第5級で、50%前後が一般的。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of speech pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of speech pays 50% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では言語機能の喪失は後遺障害第3級（100%）に該当し、シンガポールの50%より高い。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of hearing in both ears pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of hearing in both ears pays 50% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では両耳の聴力喪失は後遺障害第4級（79%）に該当。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of hearing in one ear pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of hearing in one ear pays 20% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では片耳の聴力喪失は後遺障害第9級（26%）に該当。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of a thumb pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of a thumb pays 20% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では拇指の喪失は後遺障害第9級（26%）に該当。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of any one finger pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of any one finger pays 10% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では1指の喪失は後遺障害第11級〜第13級（5%〜14%）に該当。

question: According to the Table of Compensation, loss of a big toe pays:
explanation: Table 3.1 specifies that loss of a big toe pays 5% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では足の親指喪失は後遺障害第12級（10%）に該当。

question: The aggregate of all percentage payable for any one accident shall not exceed:
choices: A) 50% of the capital sum insured
B) 75% of the capital sum insured
C) 100% of the capital sum insured
D) 150% of the capital sum insured
explanation: The aggregate of all percentage payable in respect of any one accident during the period of insurance (usually 12 months) shall not exceed 100% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも後遺障害保険金の合計は保険金額の100%が上限。

question: Some insurers pay what percentage of the capital sum insured for Permanent Total Disablement?
explanation: Some insurers' policies will pay 150% of the capital sum insured for Permanent Total Disablement.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では後遺障害保険金は100%が上限。150%支払いはシンガポール特有の高額保障。

question: The usual definition of "loss of a limb" under PA Insurance means:
choices: A) Physical severance or amputation only
B) Total and permanent functional loss of use, not necessarily physical severance
C) Loss of the limb at the shoulder or hip joint only
D) Temporary inability to use the limb for more than 12 months
explanation: Loss of limb usually means total and permanent functional loss of use, not necessarily physical severance or amputation. Loss of a hand at or above the wrist, or foot at or above the ankle.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも「用を廃した」（機能喪失）は物理的切断と同等に扱われる。

question: Some PA Insurance policies include permanent total disablement for being unable to perform how many Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)?
explanation: Some policies include injuries resulting in being permanently bedridden or being unable to perform three or more Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) as constituting permanent total disablement.
jpComparison: 日本の介護保険制度でも要介護認定にADL（日常生活動作）の評価が用いられる。3つ以上の基準はシンガポール独自。

question: The six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) defined in PA Insurance are:
choices: A) Walking, Running, Swimming, Climbing, Lifting, Bending
B) Washing, Dressing, Feeding, Toileting, Mobility, Transferring
C) Cooking, Cleaning, Shopping, Driving, Working, Exercising
D) Seeing, Hearing, Speaking, Breathing, Eating, Sleeping
explanation: ADLs are Washing, Dressing, Feeding, Toileting, Mobility, and Transferring, as specifically defined in the policy.
jpComparison: 日本の介護保険のADLも入浴・更衣・食事・排泄・移動・移乗で、シンガポールとほぼ同一の6項目。

question: Third Degree Burns covering equal to or greater than 8% of the head surface pay:
explanation: According to Table 3.1, Third Degree Burns to the head equal to or greater than 8% of surface area pay 100% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険には熱傷に対する個別の給付テーブルはなく、後遺障害等級表で判断される。
source: Ch3 §4.15, p.122 (Table 3.1, item Q(1)(a): Third Degree Burns – Head ≥8% = 100%)

question: Third Degree Burns covering equal to or greater than 20% of the body surface pay:
explanation: According to Table 3.1, Third Degree Burns to the body equal to or greater than 20% of surface area pay 100% of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では熱傷面積に基づく支払い基準はなく、障害の程度で判断される。
source: Ch3 §4.15, p.122 (Table 3.1, item Q(2)(a): Third Degree Burns – Body ≥20% = 100%)

question: Temporary total disablement benefits are typically payable for up to:
explanation: If the accident causes temporary total disablement, the insurer will pay weekly benefits, usually payable for up to 104 weeks.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の通院保険金は最大1,000日、入院保険金は最大180日が一般的。104週（約2年）はシンガポールの基準。
source: Ch3 §4.19, p.123 (Temporary total disablement weekly benefits payable for up to 104 weeks)

question: The weekly benefit for temporary total disablement is typically:
choices: A) 0.5% of the capital sum insured
B) 1% of the capital sum insured
C) 2% of the capital sum insured
D) 5% of the capital sum insured
explanation: The amount payable for temporary total disablement is lower than permanent total disablement, such as 1% of the capital sum insured per week.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の通院日額は保険金額の0.1%程度が一般的。1%/週（約0.14%/日）はほぼ同水準。
source: Ch3 §4.19, p.123 (Amount payable such as 1% of the capital sum insured per week)

question: The surgeon who cannot operate but can lecture is entitled to which benefit under PA Insurance?
explanation: The surgeon qualifies for temporary total disablement because he is unable to perform every duty pertaining to his profession (surgery), even though he can still lecture. Under PA Insurance, temporary total disablement means the insured cannot carry out all duties of their occupation, not merely some of them.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも就業不能は「通常の業務に従事できない」状態で判断され、部分的就業不能も対象。
source: Ch3 §4.20, p.123 (Surgeon unable to operate but can lecture qualifies for temporary disablement – unable to perform every duty pertaining to profession)

question: Medical expenses under PA Insurance commonly range from:
choices: A) S$500 to S$5,000 per accident
B) S$1,000 to S$15,000 per accident
C) S$5,000 to S$50,000 per accident
D) S$10,000 to S$100,000 per accident
explanation: Medical expenses cover provides a prescribed insured limit, commonly ranging from S$1,000 to S$15,000 per accident.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の治療費は入院・通院日額で支払われ、実費精算ではない点が異なる。
source: Ch3 §4.22, p.123 (Medical expenses limit commonly ranging from S$1,000 to S$15,000 per accident)

question: Some insurers separately cover mobility aids (wheelchair, crutches, etc.) up to:
explanation: Some insurers separately cover up to S$2,000, following an accident, for mobility aids such as wheelchair, crutches, ramps or railings.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険では補助具の費用は通常、治療費に含まれる。個別の上限設定はシンガポール特有。
source: Ch3 §4.24, p.124 (Some insurers separately cover up to S$2,000 for mobility aids such as wheelchair, crutches, ramps or railings)

question: Treatment by Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) physicians under PA Insurance is typically covered up to:
choices: A) 5% of the medical expenses limit
B) 10% of the medical expenses limit
C) 20% of the medical expenses limit
D) 50% of the medical expenses limit
explanation: Treatment by TCM physicians, acupuncturists, bonesetters, chiropractors, herbalists or physiotherapists is covered subject to a sub-limit, usually up to 10% of the medical expenses limit.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも整骨院・鍼灸治療は対象だが、上限割合ではなく日額で管理される。
source: Ch3 §4.25, p.124 (TCM, acupuncturist, bonesetter, chiropractor, herbalist, physiotherapist – sub-limit usually up to 10% of medical expenses limit)

question: Medical expenses under PA Insurance are payable on what basis?
choices: A) Benefit basis (fixed amount regardless of actual cost)
explanation: Medical expenses are payable on a strict reimbursement basis (indemnity), meaning contribution will apply if there is more than one policy covering such expenses.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の治療費は定額給付（日額）が主流で、実費精算ではない。シンガポールの実費精算方式とは異なる。
source: Ch3 §4.27, p.124 (Medical expenses payable on a strict reimbursement basis / indemnity; contribution applies if more than one policy)

question: Double Indemnity under PA Insurance pays double the benefit if the accident occurs while:
choices: A) The insured is at work
B) The insured is travelling as a fare-paying passenger on public transport
C) The insured is exercising at a gym
D) The insured is driving a private vehicle
explanation: Some insurers will pay double the death or PTD benefit if the accident occurs while the insured is travelling as a fare-paying passenger on public transport.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険にも「交通事故傷害特約」があり、公共交通機関利用中の事故で倍額支払いがある。
source: Ch3 §4.28, p.124 (Double indemnity: accident while travelling as a fare-paying passenger on public transport)

question: The Hospital Cash/Income benefit under PA Insurance typically provides a fixed daily benefit of:
explanation: Hospital Cash/Income provides a fixed daily benefit, e.g. S$100 per day, based on the period of hospital confinement, irrespective of actual hospital charges.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の入院日額は3,000円〜1万円が一般的。100SGD/日（約1万円）は同水準。
source: Ch3 §4.30, p.124 (Hospital Cash/Income: fixed daily benefit e.g. S$100 per day)

question: The Hospital Cash/Income benefit is payable for up to:
explanation: Hospital Cash/Income provides a daily benefit up to a certain period, e.g. 90 days. Note: the textbook qualifies this figure with 'e.g.' — actual amounts/periods may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の入院保険金は最大180日が一般的。シンガポールの90日はやや短い。
source: Ch3 §4.30, p.124 (Hospital Cash/Income payable up to a certain period e.g. 90 days)

question: Some insurers pay double the daily Hospital Cash benefit if confinement is in:
explanation: Some insurers will pay double the daily benefit if the hospital confinement is in an intensive care unit for treatment of accidental injury.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険にもICU加算（集中治療室管理加算）がある商品がある。
source: Ch3 §4.31, p.124 (Double daily benefit if hospital confinement is in an intensive care unit)

question: A recuperation benefit lump sum (e.g. S$500) is payable if hospital confinement exceeds:
explanation: Some insurers will pay a recuperation benefit in one lump sum (e.g. S$500) if the confinement in a hospital exceeds a defined period, e.g. 14 consecutive days.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険には退院後の回復給付金制度はあまり一般的ではない。
source: Ch3 §4.33, p.124 (Recuperation benefit lump sum e.g. S$500 if confinement exceeds 14 consecutive days)

question: Incremental Sum Insured rewards insureds who do not make claims by providing additional sums insured of:
choices: A) 5% of original sum insured per year
B) 10% of original sum insured per year
C) 15% of original sum insured per year
D) 20% of original sum insured per year
explanation: Many insurers reward no-claim insureds with additional sums insured at each renewal, in the form of percentages (e.g. 10%) of the original sums insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険には無事故割引はあるが、保険金額の自動増額という仕組みは一般的ではない。
source: Ch3 §4.35, p.125 (Incremental Sum Insured: additional sums in form of percentages e.g. 10% of original sums insured)

question: The incremental sum insured is typically restricted to a maximum of:
explanation: The insurer will usually restrict the additions to a maximum number of years (e.g. five years) or a maximum percentage (e.g. 50%).
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険にはこのような累積加算の仕組みは存在しない。
source: Ch3 §4.35, p.125 (Incremental additions restricted to maximum e.g. five years or maximum percentage e.g. 50%)

question: No Claim Premium Refund requires the policy to have been in force for a minimum of:
explanation: No claim premium refund is usually subject to the policy having been in force for a minimum of three consecutive years without any claim.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の無事故返戻金は保険会社によるが、3年以上の条件は一般的。
source: Ch3 §4.36, p.125 (No Claim Premium Refund: policy in force for minimum of three consecutive years without any claim)

question: PA Insurance cover is normally:
explanation: PA Insurance cover is normally worldwide.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険も国内外を問わず補償されるのが一般的。
source: Ch3 §5.1, p.125 (PA Insurance cover is normally worldwide)

question: The normal age limits for PA Insurance range from children as young as:
explanation: The normal age limits range from children from 15 days old, up to adults aged 85.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険は年齢制限が緩く、0歳から加入可能な商品もある。上限年齢は70〜80歳が一般的。
source: Ch3 §5.1, p.125 (Age limits: from children from 15 days old, up to adults aged 85)

question: Under a family PA Insurance policy, the spouse and each child typically receive benefits of:
choices: A) 10% of the insured's capital sum
B) 25% of the insured's capital sum
C) 50% of the insured's capital sum
D) 100% of the insured's capital sum
explanation: The spouse and each child may receive lower benefits, e.g. 25% of the insured's capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の家族傷害保険でも配偶者・子供の保険金額は本人より低く設定されることが一般的。
source: Ch3 §6.3, p.126 (Family policy: spouse and each child may receive lower benefits e.g. 25% of the insured's capital sum insured)

question: Under a family PA Insurance, the child education fund benefit is typically:
explanation: Some insurers will pay S$10,000 per child (with a limit of three children) in addition to the death benefit for education and tuition fees.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険には育英費用特約があるが、1子あたりの定額給付ではなく年間給付方式が多い。
source: Ch3 §6.4, p.126 (Child education fund: some insurers pay S$10,000 per child with limit of three children)

question: Which of the following is NOT a common exclusion under PA Insurance?
choices: B) Being under the influence of alcohol or drugs
explanation: Food and drink poisoning is listed as an extension (additional coverage), not an exclusion. Common exclusions include self-inflicted injury, alcohol/drugs, pregnancy, hazardous sports, and armed forces service.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも自殺・酒酔い・妊娠は免責。食中毒は特約で補償される点も類似。
source: Ch3 §7.2 p.126 (exclusions list) and §8.1 p.127 (extensions list: food and drink poisoning is an extension, not an exclusion)

question: PA Insurance excludes armed forces service EXCEPT for:
choices: B) Peacetime reservist training for national servicemen under the Enlistment Act 1970
D) Voluntary military service in foreign armies
explanation: PA Insurance excludes armed forces service and operation, except for peacetime reservist training for national servicemen in accordance with the Enlistment Act 1970.
jpComparison: 日本には徴兵制がないため同様の免責条項はない。シンガポールのNS（National Service）制度を反映した規定。
source: Ch3 §7.2, p.126 (Exclusion: armed forces service except for peacetime reservist training for national servicemen in accordance with the Enlistment Act 1970)

question: The insured must provide claim notification under PA Insurance within:
explanation: The insured must provide the insurer with a claim notification within a certain period, usually 30 days, although market practice may vary. Note: the textbook uses 'usually' / 'typically' for this figure — actual terms may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でも事故通知は「遅滞なく」が原則。30日の具体的期限設定はシンガポール特有。
source: Ch3 §9.2, p.128 (Claim notification must be provided within a certain period, usually 30 days)

question: In PA Insurance underwriting, occupations are commonly classified into how many classes?
explanation: Many insurers commonly use three classes for grouping occupations: Class I (office/non-manual), Class II (supervisory), Class III (manual work).
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険の職種級別もA級（事務職）・B級（外勤職）・C級（肉体労働職）の3区分が基本。
source: Ch3 §10.4, p.130 (Occupation classification: many insurers commonly use three classes)

question: Which occupation class has the LOWEST premium rate for PA Insurance?
choices: D) All classes have the same premium rate
explanation: Class I (professionals, office administrators) represents lower and better risks from the perspective of frequency and severity of accidents, so they have the lowest premium rates.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険でもA級（事務職）の保険料が最も低い。職種別リスク分類の考え方は共通。
source: Ch3 §10.3, p.130 (Class I professionals/office administrators represent lower and better risks → lowest premium rates)

question: PA Insurance coverage terminates upon:
choices: C) Death of the insured or payment of 100% of capital sum for permanent total disability
explanation: Coverage terminates upon death of the insured, or an injury causing permanent total disability such that compensation equals 100% (or for some insurers, 150%) of the capital sum insured.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険は保険期間満了で終了。シンガポールでは保険金100%支払いで自動終了する点が異なる。
source: Ch3 §11.1, p.131 (Coverage terminates upon death or injury causing permanent total disability with compensation equalling 100% or 150% of capital sum insured)

question: Under a per trip Travel Insurance policy, when does coverage typically end after the insured returns to Singapore?
choices: A) Immediately upon landing in Singapore
B) Up to 3 hours after return to Singapore
C) 24 hours after return to Singapore
D) At midnight on the day of return
explanation: Per trip Travel Insurance coverage ends when the insured arrives back at his residence, usually up to 3 hours after the return to Singapore. Note: the textbook uses 'usually' / 'typically' for this figure — actual terms may vary by insurer.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も自宅到着までが補償期間だが、シンガポールでは帰国後3時間以内という明確な時間制限がある点が特徴的。

question: What is the maximum length of a single trip typically allowed under a per trip Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The maximum length of a single trip allowed under a per trip policy ranges from 180 to 185 days.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険は通常最長2年まで対応可能だが、シンガポールの1回渡航型は180～185日が上限。

question: Under an annual Travel Insurance policy, what is the typical maximum duration allowed for each individual trip?
explanation: Under an annual Travel Insurance policy, each individual trip is typically limited to 90 to 92 consecutive days.
jpComparison: 日本の年間海外旅行保険でも1回の渡航期間に制限があるが、シンガポールでは90～92日が標準。

question: Under a Family Plan Travel Insurance policy, which of the following statements about children is CORRECT?
choices: A) Children can travel independently without an adult policyholder
B) Children must generally be accompanied by an insured adult on the same trip
C) Only children under age 12 are eligible for coverage
D) A maximum of two children can be covered per family
explanation: Under a Family Plan, there must usually be at least one adult travelling with the child. However, some insurers allow children to travel alone, provided they are above 17 years of age. The general rule is that children must be accompanied by an insured adult.
jpComparison: 日本の家族型海外旅行保険も家族を一括でカバーするが、子供が成人と同行する必要があるかは保険会社により異なる。シンガポールでは同行が条件。

question: What is the typical age limit for children to be covered under a Family Plan Travel Insurance policy?
choices: B) Under 18 or 21 years old, extendable to 25 if a full-time student
D) Under 30 years old if financially dependent
explanation: Children are typically eligible up to age 18 or 21, extendable to 25 if a full-time student.
jpComparison: 日本の家族特約では年齢制限が比較的緩やかだが、シンガポールでは18/21歳を基準とし、フルタイム学生なら25歳まで延長可能。

question: Which of the following is NOT a typical premium classification zone for Travel Insurance in Singapore?
explanation: Travel Insurance premiums are typically classified by three zones: ASEAN, Asia, and Worldwide. No separate European Union zone.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険は地域別で細かく保険料が分かれるが、シンガポールではASEAN・アジア・全世界の3区分。

question: At what age do Travel Insurance policyholders in Singapore typically face restricted coverage limits?
explanation: Coverage limits are typically restricted for policyholders aged 70 and above.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も高齢者は制限があるが、シンガポールでは70歳が補償制限の基準年齢。

question: What is the Personal Accident capital sum insured under a typical Deluxe Plan Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Under a typical Deluxe Plan, the PA capital sum insured is S$500,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の死亡・後遺障害補償は1,000万～5,000万円が一般的。シンガポールのデラックスプランS$500,000は日本の上位プランと同等水準。

question: What does the Double Indemnity benefit in a Travel Insurance policy provide?
choices: A) Double the medical expense coverage for overseas treatment
B) Double the PA capital sum if death/injury occurs while travelling as a fare-paying passenger on a common carrier
C) Double the baggage loss compensation for international flights
D) Double the travel delay benefit for delays exceeding 12 hours
explanation: Double Indemnity provides double the PA capital sum if death or permanent disability occurs while travelling as a fare-paying passenger on a common carrier.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には公共交通乗用具搭乗中の倍額補償はあまり見られないが、国内傷害保険では交通事故傷害特約として類似の仕組みがある。

question: What is the typical maximum limit for Medical Expenses coverage under a comprehensive Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: A comprehensive Travel Insurance policy typically provides medical expense coverage up to S$2,000,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の治療費用は無制限プランが主流。シンガポールではS$2,000,000が上限。

question: Within how many days of returning to Singapore must follow-up medical treatment commence to be covered?
explanation: Follow-up medical treatment in Singapore must commence within 31 days of return.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では帰国後の治療は通常30日以内の継続治療が条件。シンガポールの31日以内はほぼ同等。

question: What is the typical sub-limit for Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) treatment under a Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: TCM treatment is typically covered up to a sub-limit of S$750.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では漢方・東洋医学の補償は一般的でないが、シンガポールではTCMが広く利用されS$750の限度額が設定。

question: Under which condition is a Hospital Visit benefit payable under a Travel Insurance policy?
choices: A) The insured is hospitalised overseas for more than 3 days
B) The insured is hospitalised overseas for more than 5 days
C) The insured is hospitalised in Singapore for more than 7 days
D) Any hospital admission regardless of duration
explanation: The Hospital Visit benefit is payable when the insured is hospitalised overseas for more than 5 consecutive days.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険にも救援者費用特約があり3日以上の入院で家族の渡航費用が補償される。シンガポールでは5日超が条件。

question: Regarding Hospital Visit and Compassionate Visit benefits, which statement is CORRECT?
choices: A) Both benefits can be claimed simultaneously for the same event
B) Only one of the two benefits can be claimed per event - they are mutually exclusive
C) Hospital Visit is for domestic hospitalisation and Compassionate Visit is for overseas
D) Compassionate Visit covers the insured own travel, not a family member
explanation: Hospital Visit and Compassionate Visit benefits are mutually exclusive.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の救援者費用は入院見舞いと死亡時の渡航費用を包括的にカバーするが、シンガポールでは別建てかつ排他的。

question: What is the daily Hospital Confinement benefit for overseas hospitalisation under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The Hospital Confinement benefit typically pays S$200 per day overseas and S$100 per day for follow-up in Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には入院日額の概念はなく治療費実費を補償。シンガポールでは日額S$200の入院給付金が損害保険に組み込まれている。

question: What happens to the Hospital Confinement benefit when the insured is admitted to an ICU?
choices: A) The benefit remains the same as standard ward rate
D) ICU stays are not covered under Hospital Confinement
explanation: When the insured is admitted to an ICU, the Hospital Confinement benefit is typically doubled.
jpComparison: 日本の入院給付金でもICU加算がある商品は存在するが、シンガポールの旅行保険でICU時の倍額支給が標準的に組み込まれている。

question: What is the typical Emergency Medical Evacuation limit under a higher-tier Travel Insurance plan?
explanation: Under a higher-tier plan, Emergency Medical Evacuation coverage is typically unlimited.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険でも救援者費用は高額プランで無制限が一般的。シンガポールも上位プランでは無制限。

question: What is the maximum total benefit for Kidnap and Hostage coverage under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
choices: A) S$250 per day, maximum S$5,000
B) S$250 per day, maximum S$10,000
C) S$500 per day, maximum S$10,000
D) S$500 per day, maximum S$20,000
explanation: Kidnap and Hostage coverage typically pays S$250 per day, maximum S$10,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には誘拐・人質補償は通常含まれない。シンガポールがこれを標準補償に含めている点は地域のリスク認識の違いを反映。

question: What is the maximum Travel Curtailment benefit under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Travel Curtailment benefit is typically capped at S$20,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では旅行短縮費用は特約として付帯される。シンガポールのS$20,000上限は明確な上限設定。

question: What is the maximum Travel Cancellation benefit under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Travel Cancellation benefit is typically capped at S$15,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険のキャンセル費用特約は旅行代金の全額までカバーするものが多い。シンガポールはS$15,000の定額上限。

question: After how many hours of travel delay does the Travel Delay benefit become payable?
explanation: The Travel Delay benefit becomes payable after 6 consecutive hours, paying S$200 per 6 hours, max S$2,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の航空機遅延費用は6時間超の遅延で実費補償。シンガポールでは6時間ごとにS$200の定額支給。

question: What is the benefit amount for an Overbooked Flight under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The Overbooked Flight benefit pays a flat S$200.
jpComparison: 日本では航空会社のオーバーブッキングに対する補償は航空会社自身が行うのが一般的。シンガポールの旅行保険がS$200の定額給付を提供する点はユニーク。

question: What is the benefit amount for Travel Misconnection under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The Travel Misconnection benefit pays S$500 if the insured misses a connecting flight due to late arrival of prior transport.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では乗継遅延費用は実費補償が一般的。シンガポールではS$500の定額支給。

question: If the same event causes both an overbooked flight and a travel misconnection, how does the policy respond?
choices: A) Both benefits (S$200 + S$500) are payable
B) Only the higher benefit (S$500) is payable
C) Only one of the two benefits can be claimed
D) The insured may choose which to claim plus 50% of the other
explanation: If the same event gives rise to both, only one of the two benefits can be claimed.
jpComparison: 日本の保険でも同一事故に対する重複給付の制限は一般的。シンガポールではオーバーブッキングとミスコネクションの排他適用が明示されている。

question: After how many hours of delay does the Baggage Delay benefit become payable?
explanation: Baggage Delay benefit becomes payable after 6 consecutive hours, paying S$200 per 6 hours, max S$1,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の手荷物遅延費用は6時間超の遅延で実費補償。シンガポールでは6時間ごとにS$200の定額で最大S$1,000。
source: Ch4 §5.48, p.150 (Baggage Delay: every 6 consecutive hours → S$200, max S$1,000)

question: What is the typical per-item sub-limit for Personal Baggage loss or damage under a Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Personal Baggage coverage has a per-item/pair/set sub-limit of S$500.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の携行品損害も1品あたりの上限（通常10万円）がある。シンガポールのS$500は日本より低い水準。
source: Ch4 §5.49, p.151 (Personal Baggage: per-item/pair/set sub-limit S$500)

question: What is the Personal Liability coverage limit under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Personal Liability coverage is typically up to S$2,000,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の個人賠償責任は1億円が標準的。シンガポールのS$2,000,000は日本の標準より高い水準。
source: Ch4 §5.54, p.152 (Personal Liability: up to S$2,000,000)

question: The Rental Vehicle Excess benefit covers the deductible on a rental vehicle claim. From which type of agency must the vehicle be rented?
choices: A) Any vehicle rental service including peer-to-peer platforms
B) A licensed car rental agency only
C) Any agency approved by the insurer
D) Only agencies with comprehensive insurance coverage
explanation: The Rental Vehicle Excess benefit only applies when the vehicle is rented from a licensed car rental agency.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険でもレンタカーの免責額補償特約があるが、正規レンタカー会社からの借用が条件である点は同様。
source: Ch4 §5.56, p.152 (Rental Vehicle Excess: vehicle must be rented from a licensed rental agency)

question: By how many days is the policy period automatically extended if return is delayed due to transport disruption?
explanation: The policy period is automatically extended by 14 days for transport disruption.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も帰国遅延時の自動延長があるが、シンガポールの14日間自動延長は明確な基準。
source: Ch4 §6.1(a), p.153 (Automatic Extension: 14 days for transport disruption)

question: If the insured falls ill overseas and cannot return on time, by how many days is the policy automatically extended?
explanation: If unable to return due to illness, the policy is automatically extended by 30 days.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では傷病による帰国遅延の自動延長は保険会社により異なる。シンガポールの30日間自動延長は大幅に長い。
source: Ch4 §6.1(b), p.153 (Automatic Extension: 30 days for illness/injury preventing return)

question: How is GST treated for Travel Insurance premiums in Singapore?
choices: A) Standard GST rate of 9% applies
B) Reduced GST rate of 5% applies
C) Zero-rated under Section 21(3)(c) of the GST Act
D) GST-exempt as a financial service
explanation: Travel Insurance premiums are zero-rated under Section 21(3)(c) of the GST Act, as the insurance covers risks occurring outside Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本では保険料は消費税非課税。シンガポールでは海外リスクを補償する旅行保険はGSTゼロ税率という独自の扱い。非課税とゼロ税率は異なる概念。
source: Ch4 §9.5 footnote 4, p.155 (GST: zero-rated under Section 21(3)(c) of the GST Act)

question: Within how many days of returning to Singapore must a Travel Insurance claim generally be submitted?
explanation: The policy condition typically requires the insured to submit a claim within 21 or 30 days (the notification period) from the date of return to Singapore, not from the date of the insured event itself.
jpComparison: 日本の損害保険の保険金請求期限は事故発生から3年（保険法第95条）と長い。シンガポールの21～30日以内は非常に短い。
source: Ch4 §10.2, p.156 (Policy condition: claim submitted within 21 or 30 days of return)

question: Under STB insolvency protection, what is the minimum package tour cost that triggers the Travel Insurance requirement?
explanation: Travel Insurance for tour operator insolvency is triggered when the package tour costs S$1,000 or more (or deposit of S$500 or more).
jpComparison: 日本では旅行業法に基づく営業保証金制度で旅行者を保護する。シンガポールではSTBが旅行代金S$1,000以上で保険加入を条件としている。
source: Ch4 §5.34, p.148 (STB: deposit S$500 or more, or package costing S$1,000 or more)

question: Which of the following countries is typically EXCLUDED from Travel Insurance coverage?
explanation: Afghanistan is typically excluded, along with Cuba, DRC, Iran, Iraq, Liberia, Somalia, Sudan, and Syria.
